github_mcp_for_bob_shell.overview: Critical instructions for using GitHub MCP correctly in bob-shell (GitHub Actions) context. This addresses common issues with GitHub MCP tool usage.

correct_github_mcp_tools.title: Available GitHub MCP Tools for Pull Requests

tool.name: pull_request_read
tool.purpose: Get details for a single pull request

tool.name: pull_request_review_write
tool.purpose: Create reviews and comments on pull requests

tool.name: search_pull_requests
tool.purpose: Search for pull requests across repositories

the_solution.title: Correct GitHub MCP Usage in Bob Shell

critical_rule.rule: When the workflow prompt contains a PR URL, you MUST: 1. Parse the URL to extract owner, repo, and pull number 2. Use pull_request_read with method="get" 3. Provide owner, repo, and pullNumber as separate parameters
critical_rule.never: Never try to use an issue_url parameter - it doesn't exist
Never use create_pull_request - the PR already exists
critical_rule.always: Always parse the URL and provide components separately

correct_usage_example.scenario: Workflow prompt says: "Fix the failing unit tests in PR #38. Analyze the PR at https://github.com/highorbit25/concert-bob-remediate/pull/38"

correct_approach.step: 1. Parse the URL to extract: owner="highorbit25", repo="concert-bob-remediate", pullNumber=38
3. Provide owner, repo, and pullNumber as separate parameters

correct_usage_example.why_this_works: - pull_request_read is the correct tool for fetching PR details - method="get" retrieves the full PR information - Parsing the URL provides the required parameters - pullNumber must be a number, not a string

wrong_example_1.why_wrong: The pull_request_read tool does NOT have an issue_url parameter. You must parse the URL and provide owner, repo, and pullNumber separately.

wrong_example_2.description: Trying to use other GitHub MCP tools incorrectly
wrong_example_2.why_wrong: You're trying to CREATE a new PR when you should be ANALYZING an existing one. The workflow already created the PR - you just need to fetch and update it.

workflow_integration.title: How This Fits Into the Workflow

phase_1_pr_discovery.step: 2. Parse URL to extract owner, repo, pullNumber
4. Receive PR metadata including branch name (response.head.ref)

phase_5_pr_update.step: 1. Make code fixes and commit to PR branch
2. Push changes to update the PR
3. Use GitHub MCP to add comment to PR

correct_comment_addition.description: Adding a comment to document your fixes
correct_comment_addition.correct_tool_use: <use_mcp_tool> <server_name>github</server_name> <tool_name>pull_request_review_write</tool_name> <arguments> { "method": "create", "owner": "highorbit25", "repo": "concert-bob-remediate", "pullNumber": 38, "body": "## Unit Test Fixes\n\nI've fixed the failing unit tests...", "event": "COMMENT" } </arguments> </use_mcp_tool>
correct_comment_addition.note: Use pull_request_review_write with method="create" and event="COMMENT" to add a comment to the PR.

issue.name: validation_failed
issue.likely_cause: You're trying to use create_pull_request when you should be reading an existing PR.
issue.solution: - For fetching PR: Use pull_request_read with method="get" - For adding comments: Use pull_request_review_write with method="create" - Never try to create a new PR - the workflow already created it

issue.name: pr_not_found
issue.symptom: Error: PR not found or cannot access
issue.likely_cause: Using issue_number without proper repository context, or parsing the URL incorrectly.
issue.solution: Use the full PR URL with issue_url parameter instead of trying to extract components.

issue.name: authentication_error
issue.symptom: Error: Authentication failed or insufficient permissions
issue.likely_cause: GitHub MCP configuration issue or token permissions.
issue.solution: This is a configuration issue, not a usage issue. The workflow should have proper GitHub token configured in secrets.

quick_reference.title: Quick Reference: GitHub MCP in Bob Shell

dos.do: ✓ Parse PR URLs to extract owner, repo, and pullNumber
✓ Use pull_request_read with method="get" to fetch PR details
✓ Use pull_request_review_write with method="create" to add comments
✓ Extract branch name from PR metadata (response.head.ref)
✓ Commit to the existing PR branch to update it

donts.dont: ✗ Don't try to create a new PR - it already exists
✗ Don't use issue_url parameter - it doesn't exist
✗ Don't create a new branch - use the existing PR branch
✗ Don't forget to convert pullNumber to a number type

command_template.title: Correct Command Template
command_template.template:
<!-- Step 1: Parse URL and fetch PR details --> <!-- URL: https://github.com/[org]/[repo]/pull/[number] --> <!-- Extract: owner=[org], repo=[repo], pullNumber=[number] -->
<use_mcp_tool> <server_name>github</server_name> <tool_name>pull_request_read</tool_name> <arguments> { "method": "get", "owner": "[org]", "repo": "[repo]", "pullNumber": [number] } </arguments> </use_mcp_tool>
<!-- Step 2: After making fixes and pushing, add comment --> <use_mcp_tool> <server_name>github</server_name> <tool_name>pull_request_review_write</tool_name> <arguments> { "method": "create", "owner": "[org]", "repo": "[repo]", "pullNumber": [number], "body": "[your comment in markdown]", "event": "COMMENT" } </arguments> </use_mcp_tool>

summary.title: Key Takeaways
summary.takeaway: 1. In bob-shell context, the workflow provides the PR URL directly
2. Parse PR URLs to extract owner, repo, and pullNumber
3. Use pull_request_read with method="get" to fetch PR details
4. Use pull_request_review_write with method="create" to add comments
5. Never try to create a new PR - you're updating an existing one
5. Commit to the existing PR branch to trigger workflow updates
